title: Apple Computer Co. founded
description: Steve Jobs, Steve Wozniak, and Ronald Wayne signed the Apple Computer Company partnership agreement.

title: Ronald Wayne sells his stake
description: Ronald Wayne sold his 10% stake in Apple for $800, a decision that would later be worth billions.

title: Apple I goes on sale
description: The Apple I, designed by Steve Wozniak, went on sale for $666.66 through the Byte Shop.

title: Apple Computer Inc. incorporated
description: Apple Computer was formally incorporated with Mike Markkula joining as investor and co-founder.

title: Apple II unveiled at West Coast Computer Faire
description: Steve Jobs unveiled the Apple II at the first West Coast Computer Faire in San Francisco.

title: Apple II goes on sale
description: The Apple II officially went on sale for $1,298, becoming one of the first mass-market personal computers.

title: Apple Disk II released
description: Apple released the Disk II floppy drive for $495, dramatically improving the usability of the Apple II.

title: VisiCalc ships for Apple II
description: VisiCalc, the first killer app, shipped exclusively for the Apple II, driving enormous sales of the computer.

title: Jobs visits Xerox PARC
description: Steve Jobs and Apple engineers visited Xerox PARC and were shown the graphical user interface and mouse, influencing all future Apple products.

title: Apple goes public
description: Apple's IPO raised $101 million, the largest IPO since Ford Motor Company in 1956, creating more millionaires than any company in history at the time.

title: Apple III re-released
description: Apple re-released a revised Apple III after recalling the original due to serious hardware flaws.

title: Apple Lisa launched
description: Apple launched the Lisa, the first commercial personal computer with a graphical user interface, priced at $9,995.

title: John Sculley joins Apple as CEO
description: John Sculley left PepsiCo to become Apple CEO after Jobs famously asked him: 'Do you want to sell sugar water for the rest of your life?'

title: 1984 ad airs during Super Bowl
description: Apple's iconic '1984' ad directed by Ridley Scott aired during Super Bowl XVIII, widely considered the greatest commercial ever made.

title: Macintosh introduced
description: Steve Jobs unveiled the original Macintosh at Apple's annual shareholder meeting, pulling it from a bag and letting it introduce itself.

title: Apple introduces LaserWriter
description: Apple launched the LaserWriter printer alongside PageMaker, effectively creating the desktop publishing industry.

title: Steve Jobs forced out of Mac division
description: Following a boardroom power struggle with John Sculley, Steve Jobs was stripped of his role overseeing the Macintosh division.

title: Steve Jobs resigns from Apple
description: Steve Jobs resigned from Apple and announced he was founding a new computer company called NeXT.

title: Pixar spun off from Lucasfilm
description: Steve Jobs purchased the computer graphics division of Lucasfilm for $10 million, creating Pixar Animation Studios.

title: Macintosh II released
description: Apple released the Macintosh II, the first Mac with a color display and an open architecture with expansion slots.

title: Macintosh IIx released
description: Apple released the Macintosh IIx with a faster processor and FDHD floppy drive.

title: Macintosh Portable released
description: Apple released the Macintosh Portable, its first battery-powered laptop, for $6,500.

title: Macintosh Classic and LC released
description: Apple released the affordable Macintosh Classic at $999 and the Macintosh LC, dramatically expanding the Mac's market reach.

title: System 7 released
description: Apple released System 7, a major Mac OS upgrade adding virtual memory, networking, and TrueType fonts.

title: PowerBook 100 series launched
description: Apple launched the PowerBook 100, 140, and 170, defining the modern laptop form factor with a palm rest and centered trackball.

title: Apple reaches $7 billion in annual sales
description: Apple reported annual revenues exceeding $7 billion, making it the most profitable personal computer maker in the world.

title: Apple Newton MessagePad released
description: Apple released the Newton MessagePad, one of the first PDAs, pioneering handwriting recognition and mobile computing.

title: John Sculley resigns as CEO
description: John Sculley resigned as Apple CEO after a decade leading the company, replaced by Michael Spindler.

title: Power Macintosh line launched
description: Apple launched the Power Macintosh line, transitioning from Motorola 68k to PowerPC processors.

title: Apple licenses Mac OS to clone makers
description: Apple announced it would license the Mac OS to third-party manufacturers, creating the short-lived Mac clone market.

title: Copland OS project begun
description: Apple began serious development on Copland, an ambitious next-generation Mac OS that would ultimately be cancelled.

title: Michael Spindler replaced by Gil Amelio
description: Apple's board replaced CEO Michael Spindler with Gil Amelio, a semiconductor industry veteran, as the company continued to struggle.

title: Apple acquires NeXT
description: Apple announced the $429 million acquisition of NeXT, bringing Steve Jobs back to Apple and the foundation for Mac OS X.

title: Gil Amelio ousted as CEO
description: Apple's board forced Gil Amelio to resign after 500 days as CEO, with Steve Jobs stepping in as interim CEO.

title: Microsoft invests $150M in Apple
description: At Macworld Boston, Steve Jobs announced Microsoft would invest $150 million in Apple and commit to Office for Mac, shocking the tech world.

title: Steve Jobs named interim CEO
description: Steve Jobs officially became Apple's interim CEO, starting the turnaround that would make Apple the most valuable company in the world.

title: Apple Store online launches
description: Apple launched its online store at apple.com, a key part of Jobs' strategy to control the sales experience.

title: Steve Jobs kills Newton
description: Steve Jobs cancelled the Newton MessagePad product line, one of his first major decisions as interim CEO.

title: iMac G3 announced
description: Steve Jobs unveiled the colorful iMac G3 at a press event, declaring 'iMac. Hello again.' and beginning Apple's design renaissance.

title: iMac G3 goes on sale
description: The translucent Bondi Blue iMac G3 went on sale for $1,299, selling 800,000 units in its first five months.

title: iBook launched
description: Apple launched the iBook consumer laptop in a clamshell design with built-in AirPort Wi-Fi for $1,599.

title: iMac DV introduced with iMovie
description: Apple released the iMac DV with a DVD drive and bundled iMovie, launching the digital hub strategy.

title: Steve Jobs becomes permanent CEO
description: Steve Jobs dropped 'interim' from his title and became Apple's permanent CEO at Macworld Expo San Francisco.

title: Mac OS X Public Beta previewed
description: Steve Jobs previewed Mac OS X at Macworld Expo, showing the Aqua interface for the first time.

title: iTunes launched
description: Steve Jobs unveiled iTunes at Macworld Expo, calling it 'the best jukebox software in the world.'

title: Mac OS X released
description: Apple released Mac OS X 10.0 Cheetah, a complete reimagining of the Mac operating system built on a Unix foundation.

title: First Apple Retail Stores open
description: Apple opened its first two retail stores in McLean, Virginia and Glendale, California, against widespread industry skepticism.

title: iPod launched
description: Steve Jobs unveiled the original iPod, saying '1,000 songs in your pocket.' The $399 device would transform the music industry.

title: iPod goes on sale
description: The original iPod went on sale for $399, compatible only with Macs.

title: iMac G4 'lamp' released
description: Apple released the iMac G4 with an iconic floating flat-panel display on an arm, nicknamed 'the lamp.'

title: iTunes Music Store opens
description: Apple opened the iTunes Music Store with 200,000 songs at 99 cents each, selling one million songs in the first week.

title: iTunes for Windows released
description: Apple released iTunes for Windows, opening the iPod and iTunes ecosystem to the majority of PC users.

title: GarageBand introduced
description: Steve Jobs introduced GarageBand at Macworld Expo, democratizing music production for everyday users.

title: Steve Jobs reveals he has cancer
description: Steve Jobs sent an email to Apple employees revealing he had been diagnosed with a treatable form of pancreatic cancer.

title: iPod Photo released
description: Apple released the iPod Photo, the first iPod capable of displaying color photos on its screen.

title: Mac mini introduced
description: Steve Jobs unveiled the Mac mini at Macworld, a $499 headless Mac designed to attract Windows switchers.

title: Apple announces switch to Intel
description: Steve Jobs announced at WWDC that Apple would transition the Mac lineup from PowerPC to Intel processors.

title: iPod nano announced
description: Steve Jobs pulled the iPod nano out of the small pocket in his jeans, unveiling the impossibly thin flash-based music player.

title: First Intel Macs released
description: Apple released the first Intel-based MacBook Pro and iMac at Macworld, completing the Intel transition ahead of schedule.

title: Mac Pro introduced
description: Apple introduced the Mac Pro tower, the last Mac to transition to Intel, replacing the Power Mac G5.

title: iPhone announced
description: Steve Jobs announced the original iPhone at Macworld, declaring Apple was introducing 'a widescreen iPod, a phone, and an internet communicator.'

title: Apple Computer Inc. renamed Apple Inc.
description: Steve Jobs announced Apple Computer was dropping 'Computer' from its name to become Apple Inc., reflecting its expansion beyond computers.

title: iPhone goes on sale
description: The original iPhone went on sale at Apple Stores across the US, with crowds lining up for days. It sold 6 million units in its first year.

title: Mac OS X Leopard released
description: Apple released Mac OS X 10.5 Leopard with over 300 new features including Time Machine and Boot Camp.

title: MacBook Air introduced
description: Steve Jobs pulled the MacBook Air out of a manila envelope at Macworld, unveiling the world's thinnest laptop.

title: iPhone SDK launched
description: Apple released the iPhone SDK, allowing third-party developers to build apps for the iPhone for the first time.

title: App Store opens
description: Apple launched the App Store with 500 apps alongside iPhone OS 2.0, creating a new multi-billion dollar industry.

title: iPhone 3G released
description: Apple released the iPhone 3G with 3G data support and GPS for $199, making it the first truly affordable iPhone.

title: iPhone 3GS announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone 3GS at WWDC with video recording, voice control, and faster performance.

title: Steve Jobs returns from medical leave
description: Steve Jobs returned to Apple after a six-month medical leave during which he received a liver transplant.

title: iTunes goes DRM-free
description: Apple completed its transition to DRM-free music in iTunes, selling all songs without copy protection.

title: iPad announced
description: Steve Jobs unveiled the original iPad, saying it was 'a magical and revolutionary device' that sat between a smartphone and a laptop.

title: iPad goes on sale
description: The original iPad went on sale starting at $499, selling 300,000 units on its first day and 1 million within 28 days.

title: iPhone 4 announced
description: Steve Jobs unveiled the iPhone 4 at WWDC, featuring a radical new glass and stainless steel design and the Retina display.

title: iPhone 4 released
description: The iPhone 4 went on sale, with lines stretching around Apple Stores globally. It would later become famous for 'Antennagate.'

title: MacBook Air redesigned
description: Apple released a completely redesigned MacBook Air in 11 and 13-inch sizes, defining the ultrabook category.

title: Mac App Store launches
description: Apple launched the Mac App Store, bringing iPhone-style app distribution to the desktop.

title: iPad 2 announced
description: Steve Jobs made a surprise appearance to announce the iPad 2, thinner and faster with front and rear cameras.

title: iCloud announced
description: Steve Jobs announced iCloud at WWDC, replacing MobileMe and providing seamless cloud sync across all Apple devices.

title: Steve Jobs resigns as CEO
description: Steve Jobs resigned as Apple CEO, writing 'I have always said if there ever came a day when I could no longer meet my duties, I would be the first to let you know.' Tim Cook became CEO.

title: Siri and iPhone 4S announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone 4S featuring Siri, the first AI voice assistant on a smartphone, at Tim Cook's first keynote.

title: Steve Jobs passes away
description: Steve Jobs passed away at age 56 from complications related to pancreatic cancer. Apple posted a tribute reading 'Apple has lost a visionary and creative genius.'

title: iOS 5 released with iMessage
description: Apple released iOS 5 featuring iMessage, Notification Center, and iCloud integration.

title: iPad with Retina display announced
description: Apple announced the third-generation iPad with a Retina display, A5X chip, and LTE connectivity.

title: Retina MacBook Pro announced
description: Apple announced the MacBook Pro with Retina display at WWDC, the thinnest and lightest pro Mac notebook ever.

title: iPhone 5 announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone 5 with a taller 4-inch screen, Lightning connector, and LTE support.

title: iOS 6 released with Apple Maps
description: Apple released iOS 6, replacing Google Maps with Apple Maps — the launch was widely criticized for poor map data.

title: Tim Cook apologizes for Apple Maps
description: Tim Cook issued a public apology for Apple Maps' poor quality, even recommending competitor apps as alternatives.

title: iOS 7 previewed with flat redesign
description: Apple previewed iOS 7 at WWDC, a complete visual redesign by Jony Ive that replaced skeuomorphism with a flat, translucent aesthetic.

title: iPhone 5c and 5s announced
description: Apple announced the colorful plastic iPhone 5c and the iPhone 5s, the first smartphone with a fingerprint sensor (Touch ID).

title: iPad Air announced
description: Apple announced the iPad Air, 43% thinner than the iPad 3 with a dramatic reduction in size and weight.

title: Swift programming language announced
description: Apple announced Swift at WWDC, a new programming language to replace Objective-C for iOS and Mac development.

title: iPhone 6 and Apple Watch announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone 6, iPhone 6 Plus, and Apple Watch at a special event, the biggest product lineup in years.

title: iPhone 6 goes on sale
description: iPhone 6 and 6 Plus went on sale, with Apple selling 10 million units in the opening weekend, a new record.

title: Apple Pay launches
description: Apple Pay launched in the US, allowing iPhone 6 users to make contactless payments using Touch ID.

title: Apple Watch goes on sale
description: Apple Watch became available for pre-order and then went on sale starting April 24, 2015, Apple's first new product category since the iPad.

title: Apple Watch on sale in stores
description: Apple Watch became available in Apple Stores, marking the first time Apple had entered a new product category under Tim Cook.

title: Apple Music launches
description: Apple launched Apple Music, a streaming service to compete with Spotify, with a three-month free trial.

title: iOS 9 released
description: Apple released iOS 9 with a focus on intelligence and proactivity through improved Siri and Spotlight search.

title: iPhone SE announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone SE, a 4-inch iPhone with flagship internals for $399, serving users who preferred smaller phones.

title: macOS Sierra announced with Siri for Mac
description: Apple announced macOS Sierra at WWDC, bringing Siri to the Mac and renaming OS X to macOS.

title: iPhone 7 announced without headphone jack
description: Apple announced the iPhone 7, controversially removing the 3.5mm headphone jack, calling it 'courage.'

title: MacBook Pro with Touch Bar announced
description: Apple announced a redesigned MacBook Pro with a Touch Bar OLED strip replacing the function keys.

title: Apple becomes first company worth $700B
description: Apple's market capitalization surpassed $700 billion, making it by far the most valuable company in history.

title: HomePod and ARKit announced
description: Apple announced the HomePod smart speaker and ARKit augmented reality platform at WWDC 2017.

title: iPhone X announced at Steve Jobs Theater
description: Tim Cook unveiled the iPhone X at the inaugural event in the Steve Jobs Theater, featuring Face ID and an all-screen OLED design.

title: iPhone X goes on sale
description: The iPhone X went on sale for $999, the most expensive iPhone ever, with long lines at Apple Stores worldwide.

title: Apple becomes first trillion-dollar company
description: Apple became the first publicly traded US company to reach a $1 trillion market capitalization.

title: iPhone XS and Apple Watch Series 4 announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone XS, XS Max, XR, and Apple Watch Series 4 with an ECG sensor.

title: Apple TV+ and Apple Arcade announced
description: Tim Cook announced Apple TV+ streaming service and Apple Arcade gaming subscription at a special Hollywood event.

title: iPhone 11 Pro announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone 11 Pro with a triple-camera system, the first iPhone with ultra-wide photography.

title: Apple TV+ launches
description: Apple TV+ launched with original programming including 'The Morning Show' and 'See' for $4.99/month.

title: Apple closes all stores outside China
description: Apple closed all 458 retail stores outside of Greater China due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

title: Apple Silicon announced
description: Tim Cook announced at WWDC that Apple would transition Macs from Intel to its own ARM-based Apple Silicon chips.

title: iPhone 12 and 5G announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone 12 lineup with 5G support, a flat-edged design, and MagSafe charging.

title: M1 chip and first Apple Silicon Macs announced
description: Apple announced the M1 chip and first Apple Silicon Macs: the MacBook Air, MacBook Pro, and Mac mini, delivering breakthrough performance.

title: M1 iMac and AirTag announced
description: Apple announced a colorful redesigned iMac with M1 chip and the AirTag item tracker at the 'Spring Loaded' event.

title: iOS 15 and FaceTime for Android announced
description: Apple announced iOS 15 at WWDC, including the ability to send FaceTime links to Android and Windows users.

title: iPhone 13 and Apple Watch Series 7 announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone 13 lineup with a smaller notch and larger batteries, plus Apple Watch Series 7.

title: M1 Pro and M1 Max MacBook Pro announced
description: Apple announced the redesigned 14 and 16-inch MacBook Pro with M1 Pro and M1 Max chips, bringing back MagSafe and HDMI ports.

title: iPhone SE 3rd generation announced
description: Apple announced the third-generation iPhone SE with 5G support at the 'Peek Performance' event.

title: M2 chip announced
description: Apple announced the M2 chip at WWDC 2022, the first chip built on an enhanced second-generation 5nm process.

title: iPhone 14 Pro with Dynamic Island announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone 14 Pro featuring Dynamic Island, replacing the notch with an interactive pill-shaped cutout.

title: Apple becomes defendant in Epic antitrust ruling
description: A US appeals court upheld parts of a ruling requiring Apple to allow developers to link to external payment methods in apps.

title: Apple becomes first company worth $3 trillion
description: Apple briefly surpassed a $3 trillion market capitalization, the first company ever to reach that milestone.

title: Apple Vision Pro announced
description: Tim Cook unveiled the Apple Vision Pro spatial computing headset at WWDC, Apple's first major new product category in nearly a decade.

title: iPhone 15 Pro with titanium announced
description: Apple announced the iPhone 15 Pro with a titanium frame, Action button, and USB-C connectivity replacing Lightning.

title: Apple Vision Pro goes on sale
description: Apple Vision Pro went on sale for $3,499, with long lines at Apple Stores. Tim Cook was among the first customers.

title: iPad Pro with M4 chip announced
description: Apple announced the thinnest Apple product ever — the new iPad Pro with an M4 chip and Ultra Retina XDR OLED display.

title: Apple Intelligence announced
description: Apple announced Apple Intelligence at WWDC 2024, its personal AI system integrated across iPhone, iPad, and Mac.

title: iPhone 16 announced with Apple Intelligence
description: Apple announced the iPhone 16 lineup, the first iPhones designed with Apple Intelligence in mind, featuring a new Camera Control button.

title: Apple posts $120M quarterly loss
description: Apple reported a $120 million loss for Q1 1997, deepening fears the company was in terminal decline before Jobs' return.

title: Apple passes Walmart in market cap
description: Apple's market capitalization surpassed Walmart for the first time, signaling its transformation into one of America's most valuable companies.

title: iPhone trademark dispute with Cisco
description: Cisco sued Apple over the iPhone name just hours after Jobs unveiled it, as Cisco owned the iPhone trademark. The dispute was settled weeks later.

title: Safari browser announced
description: Steve Jobs announced Safari at Macworld Expo, Apple's own web browser to replace Internet Explorer as the default Mac browser.

title: iTunes tops 4 billion songs sold
description: Apple announced that iTunes had surpassed 4 billion song downloads, cementing its dominance in digital music retail.

title: Steve Jobs announces medical leave
description: Steve Jobs announced he was taking a medical leave of absence to address a hormone imbalance, with Tim Cook handling day-to-day operations.

title: PowerBook G4 Titanium unveiled
description: Steve Jobs unveiled the PowerBook G4 Titanium at Macworld, stunning the audience with its impossibly thin titanium enclosure.

title: Steve Jobs begins second medical leave
description: Steve Jobs announced a second medical leave of absence, with Tim Cook again assuming operational control of Apple.

title: iLife '06 and iWork '06 released
description: Apple released updated iLife and iWork suites alongside the first Intel Macs at Macworld Expo.

title: Apple Watch availability confirmed for early 2015
description: Apple confirmed the Apple Watch would ship in early 2015 starting at $349, with pre-orders beginning in April.

title: iPod mini announced
description: Steve Jobs announced the iPod mini in five colors for $249 at Macworld Expo, targeting a new segment of music listeners.

title: Apple reports record Q1 2013 earnings
description: Apple reported record quarterly revenue of $54.5 billion and profit of $13.1 billion for Q1 2013.

title: iTunes Store goes DRM-free
description: Apple completed its transition to DRM-free music on the iTunes Store, allowing all purchased songs to play on any device.

title: Apple settles with Qualcomm
description: Apple and Qualcomm settled their global patent dispute, ending years of litigation over modem chip licensing fees.

title: Apple settles Beatles music lawsuit
description: Apple Inc. settled a long-running trademark dispute with The Beatles' Apple Corps over the use of the Apple name and logo in music.

title: iPad name announced officially
description: Apple officially confirmed the name 'iPad' days before its launch event, ending widespread speculation about what Jobs would call his tablet.

title: Apple closes stores in China over COVID
description: Apple temporarily closed all 42 of its retail stores in China due to the coronavirus outbreak, one of the first major corporate responses to the pandemic.

title: Apple buys Beats headphones stake rumored
description: Reports emerged that Apple was in talks to acquire Beats Electronics, which would become Apple's largest acquisition at $3 billion.

title: HomePod release delayed to 2018
description: Apple announced it was delaying the HomePod smart speaker from its planned 2017 holiday release to early 2018.

title: Apple reaches $100 billion in cash
description: Apple reported holding over $100 billion in cash and investments, prompting investor calls for the company to return money to shareholders.

title: Apple opens iTunes in more countries
description: Apple expanded the iTunes Music Store to additional European countries, continuing its global digital music rollout.

title: FBI demands Apple unlock San Bernardino iPhone
description: A federal judge ordered Apple to help the FBI unlock the iPhone of a San Bernardino shooter, sparking a landmark legal battle over encryption and privacy.

title: iCloud precursor MobileMe shut down
description: Apple announced it would shut down MobileMe, its troubled cloud sync service, on June 30, 2012, to be replaced by iCloud.

title: CarPlay announced
description: Apple announced CarPlay at the Geneva Motor Show, bringing iOS integration to car dashboards from Ferrari, Mercedes-Benz, and Volvo.

title: Apple TV+ wins first award
description: Apple TV+ received its first major award nominations, marking Apple's growing presence in the entertainment industry.

title: Apple Pay expands to UK
description: Apple Pay launched in the United Kingdom, the first country outside the US to get Apple's mobile payment system.

title: Apple acquires Power Computing assets
description: Apple purchased key assets from Power Computing, the largest Mac clone maker, as part of Jobs' strategy to end the clone program.

title: iTunes tops 6 billion songs sold
description: Apple announced iTunes had surpassed 6 billion song downloads, maintaining its position as the world's largest music retailer.

title: Shuffle Bus Ad campaign begins
description: Apple launched a major advertising campaign for the iPod shuffle featuring silhouette dancers, becoming one of the most recognizable ad series of the decade.

title: Apple releases iOS 11.2.6
description: Apple released iOS 11.2.6 to fix a critical bug where a specific Telugu character would crash iPhones and iPads.

title: Verizon iPhone goes on sale
description: The iPhone 4 went on sale on Verizon for the first time, ending AT&T's exclusive US carrier deal that had lasted since 2007.

title: Time Capsule released
description: Apple released the Time Capsule, a wireless router combined with a hard drive for automatic Time Machine backups.

title: iPad preorders begin
description: Apple began taking preorders for the original iPad, with lines of interest far exceeding initial analyst expectations.

title: Apple refuses FBI order in San Bernardino case
description: Apple formally refused to comply with the FBI's court order to unlock the San Bernardino shooter's iPhone, citing the dangers of creating a backdoor.

title: Apple maps chief fired
description: Apple fired Richard Williamson, the executive responsible for Apple Maps, following the disastrous iOS 6 Maps launch.

title: Apple Watch hands-on previews published
description: Apple invited journalists for hands-on Apple Watch previews ahead of its launch, generating widespread media coverage.

title: Apple announces dividend and buyback
description: Apple announced it would pay its first dividend since 1995 and launch a $10 billion share buyback program.

title: Apple acquires Beats Music streaming
description: Reports confirmed Apple was in advanced talks to acquire Beats Electronics for $3.2 billion, its largest ever acquisition.

title: AirPods released in more countries
description: Apple expanded AirPods availability to more countries after the wireless earbuds sold out almost immediately upon their initial December 2016 launch.

title: Apple acquires Siri
description: Apple acquired Siri Inc., the makers of the Siri virtual assistant app, for a reported $200 million — a purchase that would define the next decade of iPhones.

title: Apple tops Fortune 500 most admired companies
description: Apple was named the most admired company in the world by Fortune magazine for the ninth consecutive year.

title: iPhone OS 3.0 previewed
description: Apple previewed iPhone OS 3.0, introducing copy and paste, MMS messaging, and an in-app purchase system.

title: Apple Watch pricing revealed
description: Apple revealed full Apple Watch pricing at a special event, ranging from $349 for Sport to $17,000 for Edition models.

title: Apple announces Self Service Repair
description: Apple announced Self Service Repair, allowing customers to fix their own iPhones using genuine Apple parts and tools.

title: iPhoto and iMovie for iOS released
description: Apple released iPhoto and updated iMovie for iPhone and iPad, bringing professional-grade photo and video editing to mobile devices.

title: iPhone OS 3.0 push notifications confirmed
description: Apple confirmed push notifications would arrive in iPhone OS 3.0, solving a major developer complaint about background processing.

title: Apple acquires Burstly
description: Apple quietly acquired Burstly, the company behind TestFlight app beta testing, which became the foundation of Apple's own TestFlight platform.

title: iPad 2 goes on sale
description: The iPad 2 went on sale, selling out within hours at Apple Stores across the US despite supply constraints.

title: Apple vs FBI case dropped
description: The US Justice Department dropped its legal case against Apple after the FBI found a third party able to unlock the San Bernardino iPhone without Apple's help.

title: Apple Watch pre-orders begin
description: Apple Watch pre-orders opened worldwide, with Apple Watch Sport selling out within minutes in many regions.

title: Apple acquires texture magazine app
description: Apple acquired Texture, the digital magazine subscription service, which would become the foundation of Apple News+.

title: iPhone shown at NAB convention
description: Apple demonstrated iPhone capabilities at the National Association of Broadcasters convention, showcasing its media playback features.

title: Apple Card announced
description: Apple announced the Apple Card credit card in partnership with Goldman Sachs, promising no fees, daily cashback, and a titanium physical card.

title: Apple sues HTC over iPhone patents
description: Apple filed a lawsuit against HTC alleging infringement of 20 patents related to the iPhone's user interface and hardware.

title: App Store hits 25,000 apps
description: Apple announced the App Store had surpassed 25,000 available applications, with over 800 million total downloads.

title: iPhone SDK beta released to developers
description: Apple released the iPhone SDK beta to registered developers, sparking a gold rush of app development ahead of the App Store launch.

title: Apple acquires Beats for $3.2B confirmed
description: Apple confirmed its $3.2 billion acquisition of Beats Electronics, the largest in Apple's history, gaining both a music streaming service and headphone brand.

title: New iPad and iPhone RED announced
description: Apple announced a new affordable iPad and a PRODUCT(RED) iPhone 7 at a press event, expanding its lineup for education and charity.

title: AirTag FCC filing discovered
description: FCC filings revealed the existence of AirTag before Apple's official announcement, confirming the long-rumored item tracker.

title: Apple releases COVID-19 screening app
description: Apple and CDC launched an official COVID-19 screening app to help users assess their symptoms and find testing locations.

title: iTunes reaches 25 billion songs sold
description: Apple announced iTunes had surpassed 25 billion song downloads since opening in 2003.

title: 1 billion iPhones sold milestone
description: Apple announced it had sold its one billionth iPhone, a landmark achievement Tim Cook called 'a great day for Apple.'

title: AirPods 2 released
description: Apple released AirPods 2 with a new wireless charging case and hands-free Hey Siri support.

title: Studio Display released
description: Apple released the Studio Display, a 27-inch 5K monitor with a built-in A13 Bionic chip and Center Stage camera.

title: AirPods Max ships
description: Apple began shipping AirPods Max, its $549 over-ear headphones, after strong pre-order demand.

title: New iPad sells 3 million in launch weekend
description: Apple announced the third-generation iPad sold 3 million units in its launch weekend, setting a new iPad sales record.

title: Watch OS previewed ahead of launch
description: Apple gave developers an early look at watchOS ahead of the Apple Watch launch, detailing its app architecture and APIs.

title: Apple announces $350B US investment plan
description: Apple announced a plan to invest $350 billion in the US economy over five years, creating 20,000 new jobs.

title: Apple Card sign-ups begin
description: Apple opened Apple Card sign-ups to US customers, with the physical titanium card and Apple Pay integration.

title: Apple and Google announce COVID contact tracing
description: Apple and Google announced a joint initiative to build COVID-19 contact tracing into iOS and Android using Bluetooth technology.

title: AirTag officially announced
description: Apple officially announced AirTag, its Bluetooth item tracker, at the Spring Loaded event alongside updated iMac and iPad Pro.

title: Apple acquires PrimeSense
description: Apple confirmed its acquisition of PrimeSense, the Israeli chip company behind the original Xbox Kinect sensor, fueling speculation about future AR products.

title: Apple Music tops 13 million subscribers
description: Apple Music surpassed 13 million paid subscribers less than a year after launch, making it the fastest-growing music streaming service in history.

title: Apple Pay Later announced
description: Apple launched Apple Pay Later in the US, a buy now pay later service allowing purchases to be split into four payments over six weeks.

title: Podcasts Subscriptions announced
description: Apple announced Apple Podcasts Subscriptions at its Spring Loaded event, allowing podcast creators to charge listeners for premium content.

title: iPhone OS 4.0 announced with multitasking
description: Apple previewed iPhone OS 4.0 featuring multitasking, folders, iBooks, and iAd — the most significant iPhone OS update since launch.

title: Apple acquires Shazam
description: Apple's $400 million acquisition of Shazam music recognition app was approved by European regulators, integrating Shazam into iOS.

title: Apple Store India approval granted
description: The Indian government approved Apple's application to open retail stores in India, clearing the way for the company's expansion into one of the world's largest markets.

title: Apple reaches settlement with Samsung
description: Apple and Samsung reached a final settlement in their seven-year patent war over smartphone design, with Samsung paying an undisclosed amount.

title: iTunes Radio announced
description: Apple announced iTunes Radio, a free ad-supported streaming music service, at a media event ahead of WWDC 2013.

title: Mac Studio and Studio Display released
description: Apple released the Mac Studio desktop computer and Studio Display monitor, targeting creative professionals with unprecedented performance.

title: iCloud previewed at Apple event
description: Apple gave media a first look at iCloud services ahead of WWDC 2011, including music streaming and document sync features.

title: Apple acquires Novauris Technologies
description: Apple quietly acquired Novauris Technologies, a UK speech recognition firm, bolstering Siri's voice recognition capabilities.

title: Apple Watch sells out within minutes
description: Apple Watch pre-orders sold out within minutes of going live, with delivery dates pushed to June for most models.

title: iPhone 4 prototype found in bar
description: An Apple engineer accidentally left an iPhone 4 prototype at a bar in Redwood City. Gizmodo purchased and published details, creating a massive media story months before launch.

title: Apple reports declining iPhone sales
description: Apple reported its second consecutive quarter of declining iPhone sales, raising questions about smartphone market saturation.

title: Apple acquires Emotient
description: Apple confirmed its acquisition of Emotient, a startup using AI to read emotions from facial expressions, signaling deeper AI investment.

title: Apple Card metal design revealed
description: Apple gave journalists a first look at the physical Apple Card titanium design, notable for having no visible card number or CVV.

title: Apple acquires Dialog Semiconductor assets
description: Apple agreed to acquire Dialog Semiconductor's power management chip business for $600 million, bringing key chip design in-house.

title: Apple issues $17B bond offering
description: Apple issued a record $17 billion corporate bond, one of the largest in US history, to fund its shareholder return program without repatriating overseas cash.

title: Apple acquires Emotient AI startup
description: Apple confirmed another AI acquisition as it ramped up machine learning capabilities ahead of new Siri features.

title: Apple Watch app quality praised
description: Apple Watch launched with over 3,000 third-party apps on day one, far exceeding the original App Store's launch catalog.

title: Apple Park campus opens
description: Apple began moving employees into Apple Park, its stunning new $5 billion circular campus in Cupertino designed by Norman Foster.

title: Apple passes Microsoft in market cap
description: Apple's market capitalization overtook Microsoft for the first time ever, marking a historic reversal of fortunes between the two rivals.

title: Apple acquires Beats Electronics
description: Apple officially completed its $3 billion acquisition of Beats Electronics, its largest acquisition ever, bringing Beats headphones and music streaming under the Apple umbrella.

title: Apple acquires Workflow automation app
description: Apple acquired Workflow, a popular iOS automation app, which was later relaunched as the Shortcuts app built into iOS 12.

title: Apple tops Fortune 500 list
description: Apple ranked as the most profitable company on the Fortune 500 list for the third consecutive year.

title: Apple Music details confirmed
description: Apple confirmed Apple Music subscription pricing at $9.99/month ahead of its launch, matching Spotify's standard tier.

title: iCloud subscription tiers revealed
description: Apple revealed iCloud storage pricing ahead of launch, with 5GB free and paid tiers up to 50GB.

title: Apple announces independent repair program
description: Apple announced the Independent Repair Provider program, allowing independent shops to access genuine Apple parts and tools for out-of-warranty iPhone repairs.

title: Apple vs Epic trial begins
description: The trial between Apple and Epic Games began in federal court, with Epic challenging Apple's 30% App Store commission and control over iPhone software distribution.

title: Apple acquires NextVR
description: Apple acquired NextVR, a virtual reality sports and entertainment company, for a reported $100 million, fueling speculation about Apple's AR/VR headset.

title: Apple acquires AI startup Xnor.ai
description: Apple confirmed its acquisition of Xnor.ai, which specialized in running AI models efficiently on low-power edge devices.

title: Apple announces new Mac Pro
description: Apple teased the revolutionary cylindrical Mac Pro, with Tim Cook tweeting 'Can't innovate anymore, my ass' — a response to critics of Apple's innovation.

title: Apple releases iOS 12.3 with TV app
description: Apple released iOS 12.3 featuring the redesigned TV app with Apple TV Channels, ahead of the Apple TV+ launch later in the year.

title: Apple acquires Beats officially closes
description: Apple's acquisition of Beats Electronics officially closed, with Dr. Dre and Jimmy Iovine joining Apple.

title: Apple opens first India online store
description: Apple launched its online store in India, making it easier for Indian consumers to buy Apple products directly.

title: Apple acquires Workflow app
description: Apple's acquisition of the Workflow automation app for iOS was confirmed, with the app remaining free on the App Store.

title: Apple releases iOS 13.5 with exposure notifications
description: Apple released iOS 13.5 enabling the COVID-19 exposure notification API built jointly with Google, allowing public health apps to do contact tracing.

title: Apple commits to 100% renewable energy
description: Apple announced all its US facilities ran on 100% renewable energy, ahead of its goal to achieve this globally.

title: Apple Pay reaches 1 million activations per week
description: Apple revealed Apple Pay was seeing 1 million new activations per week, with the service accepted at over 700,000 merchant locations.

title: Mac Pro design previewed
description: Apple gave media a preview of the new cheese-grater Mac Pro design ahead of its WWDC announcement, marking Apple's return to a true professional desktop.

title: Apple vs Epic trial concludes
description: The Apple vs Epic trial concluded after three weeks of testimony, with a verdict expected months later that would have mixed results for both companies.

title: Apple acquires Texture magazine app
description: Apple's acquisition of Texture, the digital magazine subscription service, was confirmed, forming the basis of Apple News+.

title: Apple reaches $2.5 trillion market cap
description: Apple's market capitalization crossed $2.5 trillion, reinforcing its position as the world's most valuable company.

title: WWDC 2020 announced as online-only
description: Apple announced WWDC 2020 would be held entirely online due to COVID-19, the first time the developer conference would not have an in-person component.

title: Mac Pro officially unveiled at WWDC
description: Apple unveiled the new modular Mac Pro in a cheese-grater design with up to 28 cores and $52,000 price tag for the fully loaded version.

title: iOS 12 announced at WWDC
description: Apple announced iOS 12 at WWDC 2018 with a focus on performance improvements, Screen Time parental controls, and Siri Shortcuts.

title: HomeKit announced at WWDC
description: Apple announced HomeKit at WWDC 2014, a framework for controlling smart home accessories from iPhone using Siri.

title: watchOS 3 announced
description: Apple announced watchOS 3 at WWDC with dramatically faster app launch times and new watch faces, addressing major performance complaints.

title: M2 MacBook Pro released
description: Apple released the M2 MacBook Pro 13-inch, the first Mac with the new M2 chip offering improved performance and battery life.

title: iOS 15 announced at WWDC
description: Apple announced iOS 15 at WWDC 2021, featuring Focus modes, redesigned notifications, FaceTime SharePlay, and Live Text.

title: HomePod officially announced
description: Apple officially unveiled the HomePod smart speaker at WWDC 2017 for $349, positioning it as an audio-first device powered by Siri.

title: Mac Pro goes on sale
description: The new Mac Pro went on sale starting at $5,999, with the Pro Display XDR monitor available for $4,999.

title: App Clips announced
description: Apple announced App Clips at WWDC 2020, allowing users to instantly use a small part of an app without downloading it.

title: iOS 12 Screen Time detailed
description: Apple detailed Screen Time, iOS 12's digital wellness feature showing how much time users spend in apps, responding to pressure from investors about iPhone addiction.

title: iOS 17 announced at WWDC
description: Apple announced iOS 17 at WWDC 2023 with StandBy mode, interactive widgets, NameDrop, and major Messages updates.

title: macOS Sierra name revealed
description: Apple revealed the name macOS Sierra at WWDC, marking the transition from OS X branding and introducing Siri to the Mac.

title: Apple Intelligence privacy details revealed
description: Apple detailed the privacy architecture of Apple Intelligence at WWDC, introducing Private Cloud Compute to process AI requests without exposing user data.

title: Apple Music launches globally
description: Apple Music launched in over 100 countries simultaneously, immediately becoming one of the most accessible music streaming services in the world.

title: Mac Pro pre-orders begin
description: Apple opened pre-orders for the new Mac Pro tower and Pro Display XDR, with delivery beginning in December 2019.

title: APFS becomes default Mac file system
description: Apple's new Apple File System became the default for Mac with macOS High Sierra, replacing HFS+ after more than 30 years.

title: M2 MacBook Air announced
description: Apple announced the M2 MacBook Air with a completely redesigned chassis, MagSafe charging, and a notched display — the biggest Air redesign since 2010.

title: Tim Cook emails staff on Brexit
description: Tim Cook sent an email to Apple staff expressing concern over Brexit's potential impact on Apple's European operations and employees.

title: App Store turns 13
description: The App Store celebrated its 13th anniversary, having grown from 500 apps at launch to over 2 million, generating hundreds of billions in developer revenue.

title: Apple Maps ground truth project expands
description: Apple confirmed its massive project to rebuild Apple Maps using its own data collection vehicles had expanded to cover all of the US.

title: Apple reaches $1.5 trillion market cap
description: Apple's market capitalization crossed $1.5 trillion during a pandemic-era tech stock surge, widening its lead as the world's most valuable company.

title: Apple hires former Burberry CEO
description: Apple hired Angela Ahrendts from Burberry as Senior Vice President of Retail, tasking her with reinvigorating the Apple Store experience.

title: Apple Music hits 11 million trial users
description: Apple revealed Apple Music had attracted 11 million trial users in its first month, a faster start than any previous music streaming service.

title: Pokemon GO drives App Store record
description: Pokemon GO's runaway success set App Store download records, with Apple benefiting massively from the augmented reality game's in-app purchases.

title: iCloud beta opens to developers
description: Apple opened iCloud beta access to registered iOS and Mac developers, ahead of the service's public launch with iOS 5 in October.

title: Apple acquires Intel modem business
description: Apple agreed to acquire Intel's smartphone modem chip business for $1 billion, giving Apple control over a critical iPhone component previously sourced externally.

title: Apple reports strong Q3 despite supply issues
description: Apple reported better-than-expected Q3 2022 results despite ongoing supply chain challenges and COVID lockdowns in China affecting production.

title: App Store small business program launches
description: Apple launched its App Store Small Business Program, reducing the commission for qualifying developers from 30% to 15%.

title: Apple Pay NFC chip confirmed in iPhone 6
description: Reports emerged confirming the iPhone 6 would include an NFC chip specifically to support Apple's forthcoming mobile payments system.

title: Apple removes VPN apps from China App Store
description: Apple removed virtually all VPN apps from the Chinese App Store following government pressure, drawing criticism from privacy advocates.

title: Apple Vision Pro pre-production ramps up
description: Reports indicated Apple had begun ramping up pre-production of Vision Pro units ahead of its early 2024 launch, with Luxshare assembling the headsets.

title: Apple Maps app detailed before iOS 6
description: Apple provided more details about its in-house Maps app ahead of the iOS 6 launch, which would controversially replace Google Maps as the default.

title: Apple reports record Q3 earnings
description: Apple reported $49.6 billion in quarterly revenue and 47.5 million iPhones sold, both quarterly records for the company.

title: Apple reports strong services growth
description: Apple reported $11.5 billion in services revenue for Q3 2019, up 13% year over year, validating its pivot toward recurring revenue streams.

title: Pokemon GO breaks App Store revenue record
description: Pokemon GO set a new record for App Store revenue in its first month, generating an estimated $200 million for Apple through its 30% commission.

title: Apple reports record Q3 despite COVID
description: Apple reported $59.7 billion in Q3 revenue, a record for the quarter, despite significant COVID-19 headwinds affecting retail stores and supply chains.

title: Apple reports best June quarter ever
description: Apple reported $81.4 billion in revenue for Q3 2021, the best June quarter in company history, driven by iPhone 12 and services growth.

title: Apple reports revenue decline amid macro headwinds
description: Apple reported its first revenue decline in years, with Q3 2022 revenues affected by supply chain disruptions and foreign exchange headwinds.

title: Apple becomes first $1T company
description: Apple's stock price hit $207.05, pushing its market cap past $1 trillion — the first US publicly traded company to reach that milestone.

title: Apple reports services record in Q3 2023
description: Apple reported $21.2 billion in services revenue for Q3 2023, an all-time record, as its software and subscription businesses continued rapid growth.

title: Apple Music reaches 11M subscribers
description: Apple Music surpassed 11 million paid subscribers after its three-month free trial period ended, converting a significant portion of trial users.

title: Apple reaches $800B market cap
description: Apple's market capitalization crossed $800 billion for the first time, continuing its march toward the historic $1 trillion milestone.

title: Apple opens first India retail store plans
description: Apple announced concrete plans to open its first official retail stores in India, the world's fastest-growing smartphone market.

title: Apple acquires Drive.ai
description: Apple acquired Drive.ai, a self-driving car startup, absorbing its engineers into Apple's secretive Project Titan autonomous vehicle effort.

title: Robin Williams passes away
description: Robin Williams passed away. Apple later honored him with a tribute in the App Store, as he was a devoted Apple fan who once met Steve Jobs.

title: Apple reaches $2 trillion market cap
description: Apple became the first US company to reach a $2 trillion market capitalization, doubling its historic $1 trillion milestone in just two years.

title: Apple Music reaches 50M subscribers
description: Apple Music surpassed 50 million subscribers globally, making it the second-largest music streaming service after Spotify.

title: Apple announces CSAM scanning feature
description: Apple announced plans to scan iCloud Photos for child sexual abuse material, sparking intense debate over privacy and encryption.

title: iPhone 5s Touch ID details leaked
description: Details of the iPhone 5s's Touch ID fingerprint sensor leaked ahead of its September announcement, previewing what would become a defining iPhone feature.

title: Apple Watch OS 2 beta seeded
description: Apple seeded watchOS 2 beta to developers, the first Apple Watch update to support native third-party apps running directly on the watch.

title: M2 MacBook Air goes on sale
description: The redesigned M2 MacBook Air went on sale globally, receiving universal praise for its fanless design, bright display, and exceptional battery life.

title: Apple acquires Intel modem unit closes
description: Apple's $1 billion acquisition of Intel's modem chip division officially closed, with over 2,200 Intel employees joining Apple.

title: Apple pay expands to Australia
description: Apple Pay launched in Australia, expanding the mobile payment service beyond North America and Europe for the first time.

title: Apple acquires Beats Music streaming confirmed
description: Apple's integration of Beats Music streaming into its ecosystem was confirmed, with the service eventually becoming Apple Music.

title: Apple releases iOS 10.3.3
description: Apple released iOS 10.3.3, dropping support for 32-bit apps and warning users that older apps would not work in future iOS versions.

title: Apple reaches $500B market cap again
description: Apple's market cap recovered to $500 billion after declining from its 2012 peak, as iPhone 5s anticipation drove investor confidence.

title: Apple removes Alex Jones podcasts
description: Apple removed Infowars podcasts from iTunes and the Podcast app, citing violations of its hate speech policies, ahead of similar actions by other platforms.

title: Apple acquires Turi machine learning company
description: Apple acquired Turi, a machine learning startup, for a reported $200 million to bolster its AI and CoreML capabilities.

title: Apple announces Lockdown Mode
description: Apple announced Lockdown Mode in iOS 16, an extreme security setting for users at high risk of sophisticated cyberattacks.

title: Apple Music grows to 15M subscribers
description: Apple Music reached 15 million subscribers in just two months of availability, growing faster than any previous music service.

title: Mac Pro pre-orders confirmed for fall
description: Apple confirmed the new Mac Pro tower would be available to order in fall 2019, with configurations starting at $5,999.

title: Apple delays return to office
description: Apple delayed its planned return-to-office date for the third time due to COVID-19, pushing the full return to early 2022.

title: Apple launches iPhone XS pre-production
description: Reports indicated Apple had begun pre-production of the iPhone XS lineup ahead of its September announcement event.

title: Fortnite removed from App Store
description: Apple removed Fortnite from the App Store after Epic Games deliberately violated App Store payment rules, triggering the landmark Apple vs Epic legal battle.

title: Apple announces iPhone 14 event
description: Apple officially announced its 'Far Out' September 7 event, confirming the iPhone 14 lineup reveal with invites featuring a cosmic theme.

title: iPad Pro display details leaked
description: Details of the iPad Pro's 12.9-inch display leaked ahead of its announcement, confirming Apple's move into the large tablet market.

title: Apple acquires majority of Intel modem team
description: Apple completed integration of over 2,200 Intel modem engineers into its hardware team, accelerating development of its own 5G modem chip.

title: iPhone X name confirmed in HomePod firmware
description: The name 'iPhone X' and Face ID details were accidentally revealed in leaked HomePod firmware, one of the biggest Apple leaks in years.

title: iPhone 7 pre-orders begin
description: Pre-orders for the iPhone 7 and 7 Plus opened, with the Jet Black model selling out within minutes and delivery dates pushed to weeks after launch.

title: Apple One subscription bundle announced
description: Apple announced Apple One, a bundle combining Apple Music, TV+, Arcade, and iCloud storage into a single subscription starting at $14.95/month.

title: Apple Card begins US rollout
description: Apple began the general public rollout of Apple Card to US customers, with the physical titanium card and Daily Cash rewards.

title: iPad Pro officially announced
description: Apple officially announced the iPad Pro with a 12.9-inch display, Apple Pencil, and Smart Keyboard at a September special event.

title: iPhone XS pre-orders break records
description: iPhone XS and XS Max pre-orders set new records, with Apple's website experiencing significant traffic on launch day.

title: iOS 15 released
description: Apple released iOS 15 to the public, featuring FaceTime improvements, Focus modes, Live Text, and redesigned notifications.

title: iPhone 7 goes on sale
description: iPhone 7 and 7 Plus went on sale globally with massive demand for the Jet Black finish, despite controversy over the missing headphone jack.

title: Apple Watch Series 6 released
description: Apple Watch Series 6 with blood oxygen monitoring went on sale, alongside the new budget-friendly Apple Watch SE.

title: iOS 11 released
description: Apple released iOS 11 with a redesigned Control Center, drag and drop on iPad, ARKit augmented reality support, and a new Files app.

title: Apple TV+ launch details revealed
description: Apple confirmed Apple TV+ would launch November 1 at $4.99/month, with a free year offered to customers buying new Apple devices.

title: iPhone 14 Pro goes on sale
description: iPhone 14 Pro and Pro Max went on sale featuring Dynamic Island, the always-on display, and 48MP main camera.

title: iPhone XS and XS Max on sale
description: iPhone XS and XS Max went on sale with Apple's best-ever dual camera system and the A12 Bionic chip.

title: Apple Watch Series 7 announced
description: Apple announced the Apple Watch Series 7 with a larger, more crack-resistant display and faster charging at a September event.

title: iPhone 15 goes on sale
description: The iPhone 15 lineup went on sale, featuring USB-C for the first time, a Dynamic Island on all models, and a 48MP camera on base models.

title: Apple One subscription bundle launches
description: Apple One subscription bundles became available in 100+ countries, offering Apple Music, TV+, Arcade, and iCloud storage at a discount.

title: Apple Pay launched
description: Apple Pay officially launched in the US with support from 220,000 merchants and six major banks, enabling tap-to-pay from iPhone 6.

title: iPhone X pre-orders open
description: iPhone X pre-orders opened at 12:01AM PT, with delivery dates immediately pushed to 5-6 weeks as demand overwhelmed supply.

title: iPhone 15 Pro demand exceeds supply
description: Apple reported iPhone 15 Pro and Pro Max delivery times stretched to 5-6 weeks globally due to strong demand and constrained supply of titanium components.

title: Mac Pro production begins in Texas
description: Apple confirmed the new Mac Pro was being assembled in Austin, Texas, continuing a tradition of US manufacturing for its pro desktop.

title: iPad Pro shipping confirmed for November
description: Apple confirmed the iPad Pro and Apple Pencil would begin shipping in November 2015, with the Smart Keyboard arriving later.

title: Apple releases OS X Yosemite
description: Apple released OS X Yosemite with a major visual redesign inspired by iOS 7, Continuity features, and iCloud Drive.

title: Apple announces iPhone 12 availability
description: Apple opened iPhone 12 and 12 Pro pre-orders, with the standard iPhone 12 going on sale October 23 and Pro Max in November.

title: HomePod mini goes on sale
description: Apple's HomePod mini went on sale for $99, offering Siri, intercom, and whole-home audio at an accessible price point.

title: Apple announces M2 Pro and M2 Max Macs
description: Apple announced updated MacBook Pro models with M2 Pro and M2 Max chips alongside a new Mac mini with M2 at a virtual event.

title: MacBook Pro with Touch Bar revealed
description: Apple unveiled the redesigned MacBook Pro with Touch Bar at a special event, also introducing USB-C as the only port.

title: iPad Pro goes on sale
description: The iPad Pro, Apple Pencil, and Smart Keyboard went on sale, with Apple positioning the Pro as a laptop replacement for professionals.

title: M3 chips and MacBook Pro announced
description: Apple announced M3, M3 Pro, and M3 Max chips at a Scary Fast event, alongside new MacBook Pro and iMac models built on 3nm process.

title: AirPods 3 announced
description: Apple announced AirPods 3 with spatial audio and adaptive EQ, adopting the AirPods Pro design language for the standard model.

title: Macbook Pro with Touch Bar goes on sale
description: The new MacBook Pro with Touch Bar went on sale, though its high price and limited ports drew significant criticism from pro users.

title: AirPods Pro announced
description: Apple announced AirPods Pro with active noise cancellation, a customizable fit, and transparency mode for $249.

title: MacBook Pro 14 and 16 go on sale
description: The redesigned MacBook Pro 14 and 16-inch with M1 Pro and M1 Max went on sale, widely praised as the best Mac laptops ever made.

title: Apple reports record services revenue
description: Apple reported $19.2 billion in services revenue for Q4 2022, a new quarterly record driven by App Store, Apple Music, and iCloud subscriptions.

title: iPad Pro with Face ID announced
description: Apple announced the redesigned iPad Pro with Face ID, USB-C, and no headphone jack — the most significant iPad redesign in years.

title: Apple acquires Swell Radio app
description: Apple acquired Swell, an AI-powered radio and podcast app, boosting its podcast and audio streaming capabilities.

title: AirPods Pro goes on sale
description: AirPods Pro went on sale for $249, immediately selling out in Apple Stores worldwide due to overwhelming demand.

title: iPhone X sells out instantly
description: iPhone X sold out online within minutes of pre-orders opening, with delivery estimates pushed to December for most buyers.

title: MacBook Air with Retina display released
description: Apple released a redesigned MacBook Air with a Retina display and Touch ID for $1,199, the first major Air redesign in five years.

title: HomePod mini colors announced
description: Apple announced new HomePod mini colors including yellow, orange, and midnight, expanding the smart speaker lineup.

title: MacBook Pro Touch Bar goes on sale
description: The MacBook Pro with Touch Bar became available in stores, with Apple also releasing the non-Touch Bar 13-inch model.

title: Apple releases iOS 16.1
description: Apple released iOS 16.1 with Live Activities, iCloud Shared Photo Library, and Matter smart home support.

title: Apple TV+ wins first Emmy nomination
description: Apple TV+'s 'The Morning Show' received its first Emmy nomination, validating Apple's investment in original programming.

title: M1 Macs go on sale
description: The first Apple Silicon Macs — MacBook Air, MacBook Pro 13, and Mac mini — went on sale, immediately receiving stunning reviews for performance and battery life.

title: iPad Pro with Face ID goes on sale
description: The new iPad Pro with Face ID and USB-C went on sale, with reviewers praising its performance but noting the high price.

title: AirPods 3 goes on sale
description: AirPods 3 went on sale for $179 with spatial audio, adaptive EQ, and IPX4 water resistance.

title: Apple Pay expands to more banks
description: Apple Pay expanded support to dozens more US banks and credit unions, growing the number of eligible cards to over 500.

title: Apple Watch Ultra goes on sale
description: Apple Watch Ultra went on sale for $799, targeting extreme athletes with its titanium case, largest battery ever, and dual GPS.

title: Apple releases iOS 17.1
description: Apple released iOS 17.1 with AirDrop improvements, NameDrop refinements, and the return of the always-on display clock during charging.

title: Apple Silicon transition detailed
description: Apple detailed the full two-year plan for transitioning all Mac models from Intel to Apple Silicon chips, with the first three models already available.

title: Mac Pro pre-orders open
description: Apple opened pre-orders for the new Mac Pro and Pro Display XDR ahead of their December delivery.

title: Apple releases iOS 15.1.1
description: Apple released iOS 15.1.1 fixing call drop issues specifically affecting iPhone 12 and 13 models.

title: Apple Watch saves man having heart attack
description: A man in the US reported that his Apple Watch detected an irregular heartbeat that led to doctors discovering he was having a heart attack, one of the first publicized life-saving Apple Watch stories.

title: MacBook Pro Touch Bar reviewed
description: Major tech publications published their MacBook Pro Touch Bar reviews with mixed reactions — praising the hardware but questioning the Touch Bar's utility.

title: Apple releases macOS Big Sur
description: Apple released macOS Big Sur, the biggest Mac visual redesign in nearly two decades, with a new design language shared with iOS and support for Apple Silicon.

title: Apple launches iPhone trade-in program expansion
description: Apple expanded its iPhone trade-in program with higher values and instant credit at Apple Stores, simplifying the upgrade process.

title: Foxconn protests disrupt iPhone production
description: Workers at Foxconn's Zhengzhou iPhone factory protested over unpaid bonuses and COVID restrictions, threatening holiday iPhone supply.

title: Apple Watch becomes FDA-cleared medical device
description: Apple Watch's ECG and irregular rhythm notification features received expanded FDA clearance, cementing its status as a legitimate medical device.

title: iMac Pro announced for December
description: Apple confirmed the iMac Pro would go on sale in December 2017 starting at $4,999, as its most powerful all-in-one computer ever.

title: Apple releases Security Response updates
description: Apple deployed Rapid Security Response updates for the first time, a new mechanism to patch critical vulnerabilities without a full OS update.

title: Apple releases macOS Big Sur update
description: Apple released macOS 11.0.1 fixing issues some users experienced during the initial Big Sur launch, including boot problems on older Macs.

title: Apple releases iOS 16.2 with Advanced Data Protection
description: Apple released iOS 16.2 featuring Advanced Data Protection, enabling end-to-end encryption for iCloud backups for the first time.

title: Apple acquires classical music app Primephonic
description: Apple confirmed its acquisition of Primephonic, a classical music streaming service, later integrated into Apple Music Classical.

title: Mac Pro goes on sale
description: The new Mac Pro tower and Pro Display XDR went on sale, with the base configuration starting at $5,999.

title: AirPods Max announced
description: Apple surprised everyone by announcing AirPods Max, its $549 over-ear headphones with active noise cancellation and spatial audio, with no prior leaks.

title: Apple releases M2 Mac mini
description: Apple released the M2 and M2 Pro Mac mini, dramatically improving performance while lowering the starting price to $599.

title: iMac Pro pre-orders begin
description: Apple opened pre-orders for the iMac Pro, its most powerful Mac ever, ahead of its December shipping date.

title: Apple releases iOS 17.2 with Journal app
description: Apple released iOS 17.2 introducing the Journal app for personal reflection, along with Action Button improvements and satellite emergency messaging for iPhone 14.

title: Cyberpunk 2077 tops App Store charts on launch
description: Major game releases drove record App Store revenues during the holiday season, with Apple taking 30% of all in-app purchase revenue.

title: Apple releases macOS Catalina 10.15.2
description: Apple released macOS Catalina 10.15.2 with Apple Music improvements and bug fixes, the first significant update to the annual macOS release.

title: Apple Watch Ultra sells out for holidays
description: Apple Watch Ultra sold out ahead of the holidays as gift demand exceeded supply for the premium adventure-focused smartwatch.

title: Apple releases iOS 15.2 with legacy contacts
description: Apple released iOS 15.2 with Digital Legacy contacts allowing trusted people access to your account after death, plus App Privacy Report.

title: Apple HomePod reaches 6% smart speaker market share
description: Reports indicated HomePod had captured 6% of the smart speaker market despite its premium price, showing moderate consumer interest.

title: App Store holiday season begins record stretch
description: Apple reported the App Store holiday season was on track for record revenues with New Year's Day becoming the single biggest day for App Store spending.

title: Apple releases AirPods firmware update
description: Apple released a firmware update for AirPods Pro improving conversation boost and personalized spatial audio accuracy.

title: iMac Pro goes on sale
description: The iMac Pro went on sale starting at $4,999, the most powerful Mac desktop Apple had ever sold.

title: Apple releases iOS 13.3
description: Apple released iOS 13.3 with Communication Limits for Screen Time and security key support for two-factor authentication.

title: Apple releases iOS 9.2
description: Apple released iOS 9.2 with Apple Music improvements, Safari fixes, and support for the iPhone Lightning to 3.5mm adapter.

title: Apple releases iOS 14.3 with Fitness+
description: Apple released iOS 14.3 launching Apple Fitness+, its workout subscription service integrated with Apple Watch.

title: Apple hits $3 trillion market cap briefly
description: Apple's stock briefly crossed the $3 trillion market cap mark intraday, the first company ever to reach that valuation.

title: App Store sets single-day spending record
description: Christmas Eve set a new single-day App Store spending record as users activated new devices and purchased apps and subscriptions as gifts.

title: App Store Christmas Day record broken
description: Christmas Day 2016 became the biggest single day in App Store history with over $240 million spent on apps and in-app purchases.

title: App Store New Year's record incoming
description: Apple reported the week between Christmas and New Year's was on track to be the biggest App Store week ever, with users activating millions of new devices.

title: Apple releases iOS 13.3.1 beta
description: Apple released the first beta of iOS 13.3.1 to developers, with fixes for the UWB chip and FaceTime issues.

title: Apple reports holiday iPhone activations surge
description: Data firms reported record iPhone activations on Christmas Day 2021, with Apple estimated to have activated over 30 million devices during the holiday week.

title: Apple Music Classical app announced
description: Apple announced Apple Music Classical, a dedicated classical music streaming app built on the Primephonic acquisition, launching in early 2023.

title: Apple ends year as most valuable company
description: Apple closed 2020 as the world's most valuable company with a market cap of $2.25 trillion, up over $800 billion during the pandemic year.

title: App Store breaks New Year's spending record
description: New Year's Day 2020 (the following day) broke the all-time single-day App Store spending record at over $386 million, capping Apple's best holiday season ever.
